_README: SAMPLE -- the 'hooks' block below is directly installable on the Windows workspace (Windows PowerShell 5.1 + `python` on PATH; `pwsh`/`python3` are NOT required). Copy the whole 'hooks' object into the project's .claude/settings.json (or merge it). Project-level Claude config (.claude/**) is a protected path under this repo's policy, so the agent does NOT install this file itself; jlcbk owns the install. The committed rollout default is 'audit' (scripts/hooks/config.json), so installing this sample alone blocks NOTHING until the mode is raised. We use EXEC FORM (command + args) with ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}, the form the official Claude Code hook docs recommend for path placeholders: no shell is involved, so there are no quoting/pwsh/python3 pitfalls on Windows.

_non_windows_alternative._note: macOS/Linux (bash, python3 on PATH). Shell form; keep separate from the Windows config above. Do not mix into the Windows 'hooks' object.

_windows_powershell_wrapper_alternative._note: Optional Windows alternative that goes through the .ps1 wrappers via Windows PowerShell 5.1 (powershell.exe). Use only if you prefer a PowerShell entrypoint over the direct 'python' exec form in 'hooks'. pwsh (PowerShell 7) is NOT required.

_enabling_modes.audit: default; no change needed. Findings only.
_enabling_modes.pretool-enforce: set SELF_EVO_ROLLOUT_MODE=pretool-enforce, or set rollout_mode in scripts/hooks/config.json. PreToolUse blocks; Stop stays advisory.
_enabling_modes.full-enforce: set SELF_EVO_ROLLOUT_MODE=full-enforce. Both hooks may block. Enable only after reviewing audit findings.

_issue_override: Set SELF_EVO_ISSUE=<number> to tell the Stop hook which issue a run targets when it cannot be read from the agent branch name (e.g. a branch not named '<N>-...'). Set SELF_EVO_BASE_REF=<ref> to override the validator's PR base ref.
_rollback: Set SELF_EVO_ROLLOUT_MODE=audit, or delete the 'hooks' block from .claude/settings.json and restart Claude Code. SELF_EVO_STOP_GUARD=1 escapes a Stop loop.
